title: Fast Fall Meal Ideas
notes: Collection of go-to meals from when kids were young

Fast Side Dishes section includes: Couscous (cook in broth or water with seasonings), Israeli or Pearl Couscous, fast cooking quinoa, Ratatouille from Harmon's deli, Alexia's sweet potato fries with Cavendar's Greek seasoning
Roasted Vegetables: Line roasting pan with aluminum foil, preheat oven to 425 degrees (use convection setting if available). Roasting brings out the flavor.
Cauliflower or Broccoli combination: Drizzle with orange juice, EVOO, salt and pepper. Bake 15-18 minutes.

Root vegetables combination: Chop fingerling potatoes, red potatoes, sweet potatoes, turnips, celery root, celery, kohlrabi bulbs, beets, onions, or whole garlic cloves. Drizzle with EVOO, salt and pepper. Bake 40 minutes.
Squash combination: Chop butternut squash, zucchini or other squash (or buy pre-chopped). Drizzle with EVOO, press 2-3 garlic cloves on top. Bake 30 minutes.
French Dip note: Can caramelize 2 sliced onions in butter on low-medium heat until golden, or use caramelized onions from the can for a fancier version

Panini combinations: 1) Ciabatta, basil pesto, goat cheese, red pepper strips 2) Ciabatta, chicken, marinara sauce, mozzarella or provolone 3) Firm sandwich bread, turkey, artichoke pesto, tomatoes, Havarti cheese 4) Caprese with fresh mozzarella, basil, tomato, balsamic 5) Prosciutto, fontina or Coastal cheese, spinach 6) Pizza panini with tomato/pizza sauce, fresh mozzarella, basil
French Omelet Bar: Set up filling choices - bacon, ham, chopped tomatoes, chopped green onions, spinach, grated cheeses, bay shrimp, sliced avocados, chopped vegetables
Cheese Strata variations: add bacon, sausage, ham, chopped spinach, or sautéed mushrooms in the middle of bread
Leftover meat idea: Use with puff pastry shells and leftover roast or chicken in cream of mushroom soup mixture
